name: Santa Maria de Martorelles Springs Route
name_original: Santa Maria de Martorelles, Font de la Mercè, Font de la Teula, Font del Ferro – Ruta Circular
description: Jog the moderate 4.3-mile Santa Maria de Martorelles Springs Route, exploring natural springs and diverse forests in just under an hour.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Santa Maria De Martorelles traverse a landscape characterized by the gentle slopes and Mediterranean forests of the Parc de la Serralada Litoral. The region features a mix of vineyards, pine, and holm oak forests, providing varied terrain for runners. Numerous natural springs and small hills contribute to the area's diverse natural features. This municipality offers a blend of agricultural charm and refreshing "water landscapes." Best jogging routes around Santa Maria De Martorelles The most popular jogging route is Santa Maria de Martorelles Springs Route, a 4.3 miles (7.0 km) moderate trail that takes about 50 minutes to complete. This route guides runners through various natural springs, including Font del Ferro and Font de la Mercè. Another top favourite among local runners is Gravel Parc – Turó d'en Galceran Viewpoint loop from Poblat ibèric de Castellruf, a difficult 6.3 miles (10.2 km) path. This route offers panoramic views from Mirador del Turó d'en Galceran and passes by archaeological remains at Turó de Castellruf. Local runners also love the Turó d'en Galceran Viewpoint – La Mercè Spring loop from Santa Maria de Martorelles, a 7.7 miles (12.5 km) trail leading through forests and past natural springs, often completed in about 1 hour 30 minutes. Jogging around Santa Maria De Martorelles is defined by Mediterranean forests, vineyards, and natural springs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging runs with elevation changes. The routes in Santa Maria De Martorelles are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 200 reviews. More than 20,000 runners have used komoot to explore Santa Maria De Martorelles's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Santa Maria de Martorelles?
A: Santa Maria de Martorelles boasts a vast network of over 450 running routes. These trails cater to all levels, with options ranging from easy strolls to more challenging runs with significant elevation changes.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Santa Maria de Martorelles?
A: The jogging trails in Santa Maria de Martorelles offer a diverse landscape. You'll run through the gentle slopes and Mediterranean forests of the Parc de la Serralada Litoral, encountering vineyards, pine, and holm oak forests. The area is also known for its refreshing 'water landscapes' with numerous natural springs.

Q: Are there any easy running routes for beginners in Santa Maria de Martorelles?
A: Yes, there are approximately 25 easy running routes in Santa Maria de Martorelles, perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed jog. These paths allow you to enjoy the natural beauty without significant difficulty.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Santa Maria de Martorelles?
A: Absolutely. Many of the routes in the region are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e964007945/santa-maria-de-martorelles-springs-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Maria de Martorelles Springs Route&lt;/a&gt;, which is a popular moderate circular trail guiding you through various natural springs.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints accessible from the running trails?
A: For breathtaking panoramic views, consider routes that pass by the Mirador del Turó de Galzeran. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32008012" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gravel Parc – Turó d'en Galceran Viewpoint loop from Poblat ibèric de Castellruf&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging option that offers spectacular vistas over the Vallès and Maresme regions.

Q: Are there any historical or archaeological sites along the running paths?
A: Yes, the area is rich in history. You can explore routes that lead to significant archaeological remains such as the Iberian settlement, dolmen, and menhir at Turó de Castellruf. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33954248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turó d'en Galceran Viewpoint – Castellruf Dolmen loop from Santa Maria de Martorelles&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that passes by the Dolmen de Castellruf.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Santa Maria de Martorelles?
A: The running routes in Santa Maria de Martorelles are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.5 out of 5 stars from over 250 reviews. Runners frequently praise the varied terrain, the beauty of the natural springs, and the well-maintained paths within the Parc de la Serralada Litoral.

Q: Are there any routes that feature natural springs or water landscapes?
A: Yes, the region is known for its 'water landscapes' and numerous natural springs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e964007945/santa-maria-de-martorelles-springs-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Maria de Martorelles Springs Route&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to take you past several springs like Font del Ferro and Font de la Mercè, offering a refreshing element to your run.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Santa Maria de Martorelles?
A: Santa Maria de Martorelles enjoys a Mediterranean interior climate with mild winters and hot, dry summers. This makes it suitable for outdoor pursuits for much of the year, though spring and autumn offer moderate rainfall and pleasant temperatures, ideal for running.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes for advanced runners?
A: For advanced runners seeking a challenge, Santa Maria de Martorelles offers approximately 185 difficult routes. These often feature significant elevation changes and more technical paths. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33955896" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turó d'en Galceran Viewpoint – La Mercè Spring loop from Santa Maria de Martorelles&lt;/a&gt;, a 12.5 km trail with considerable ascent.

Q: Can I combine my run with a visit to a historical landmark?
A: Certainly! Many running routes in the area pass by historical landmarks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32199627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellruf Dolmen – Teula Fountain loop from Poblat ibèric de Castellruf&lt;/a&gt; allows you to experience both natural springs and the ancient Dolmen de Castellruf during your run.
tips:
La Font de la Mercè is a fountain found in the Can Girona valley of the Serralada Litoral Park.

   The source is born from two mines located at a slightly higher height. The water is not drinkable, it flows almost all year round and is collected in a tank, located a few meters from the source, which is channeled to the Can Girona residence. The fountain has a masonry wall lined with slate, and the water flows through a metal spout. The sink is semicircular in shape, collects the water in a small pool and a sink returns it to the ditch below. The place was restored in 1993.
This is an area with a lot of brush, you will have to be careful not to miss anything, since some sources are difficult to find.

The Font del Ferro is a natural spring. It is a ferruginous spring that springs from under two torrents: the Sot dels Verreus and the Sot dels Cirerers, which come together at this point and form the Font Sunyera torrent. As it is a natural spring, in times of drought this spring only drips or is dry, but it flows quite a lot of water in rainy seasons. In front of the fountain there is a small specimen of micaquer (Japanese medlar), very common in the patios and gardens of the towns on both sides of the Park but unusual in the middle of the forest.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog the Santa Maria de Martorelles Springs Route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 7 km long. For jogging, it typically takes around 50 minutes to an hour, depending on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery. The official tour data suggests an average duration of about 51 minutes.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner joggers?
faqs.answer: The Santa Maria de Martorelles Springs Route is classified as moderate. While it's not excessively long, it involves an elevation gain of over 240 meters, which can be challenging for absolute beginners. Joggers with a good basic fitness level will find it enjoyable, but beginners might prefer to walk or tackle shorter sections.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The route conveniently starts from the church square in Santa Maria de Martorelles. You should be able to find parking in or around the village center, close to the starting point.

faqs.question: Is the Santa Maria de Martorelles Springs Route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is generally dog-friendly. However, as it passes through the Parc Natural de la Serralada del Litoral, it's important to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all trail users. Always remember to clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Beyond the beautiful natural landscapes, the route is rich in natural and historical highlights. You'll visit several natural springs like La Mercè Spring, Teula Fountain, and Font del Ferro. Additionally, you'll encounter significant archaeological sites such as the Dolmen de Castellruf, the Menhir de Castellruf, and the remains of the Iberian settlement of Castellruf, offering panoramic views of the Vallès region.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular path that primarily follows accessible dirt tracks, especially after leaving the urban center. Some sections may require sure-footedness due to eroded granite (sauló). You'll pass through diverse ecosystems including vineyards, riverine forests, and holm oak groves, and follow torrents like the Sunyera and Can Girona.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Parc Natural de la Serralada del Litoral?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Parc Natural de la Serralada del Litoral, where this route is located. However, visitors are expected to respect park regulations, such as staying on marked paths, not disturbing wildlife, and taking all litter with them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best times to jog this route. The weather is milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant. In spring, the springs are more likely to be flowing consistently after winter rains. Summer can be very hot, so if you go then, it's best to start early in the morning or late in the afternoon to avoid the midday heat.

faqs.question: Do the springs always have water flowing?
faqs.answer: The flow of the springs along the route is dependent on rainfall. While some, like La Mercè Spring, tend to flow more consistently, others may be reduced to a trickle or even dry during prolonged periods of drought, especially in the drier summer months. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water with you.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other official trails. Notably, it follows the entire 'Les fonts de Santa Maria de Martorelles' trail. It also briefly intersects with parts of 'De la Tordera al Llobregat. Variant Palaudaries - Alella', 'Camí d'Alella', and 'Castellruf'.
